resultResponses.name: Normal CBC/CMP
resultResponses.text: [PATIENT_NAME]'s blood work was reviewed and is overall reassuring for age. No change to the current plan is needed at this time. Please continue routine follow up.

resultResponses.name: Normal lead screen
resultResponses.text: Lead screening for [PATIENT_NAME] is within normal range. Continue routine preventive care and risk reduction practices at home.

resultResponses.name: Mild anemia message
resultResponses.text: [PATIENT_NAME]'s labs show mild anemia. Please increase iron-rich foods and we will recheck in follow-up as discussed. Contact us sooner for worsening fatigue, pallor, or other concerns.

resultResponses.name: Positive strep culture
resultResponses.text: Throat testing is positive for strep. Please complete the full antibiotic course as prescribed, encourage hydration, and call if fever/pain are not improving over the next 48 hours.

resultResponses.name: Positive viral test
resultResponses.text: [PATIENT_NAME]'s viral test returned positive. Treatment is supportive: fluids, rest, and symptom control. Please seek care for breathing difficulty, dehydration, or worsening symptoms.

messageReplies.name: Fever home triage
messageReplies.text: For [PATIENT_NAME], continue fluids and age-appropriate fever medication as needed for comfort. Seek urgent care for breathing difficulty, persistent vomiting, poor urine output, unusual sleepiness, or fever lasting more than 5 days.

messageReplies.name: Cough triage
messageReplies.text: For [PATIENT_NAME], use supportive care (fluids, humidified air, saline as needed). Please seek urgent evaluation for increased work of breathing, chest pain, dehydration, or worsening symptoms.

messageReplies.name: Rash triage
messageReplies.text: Please monitor [PATIENT_NAME]'s rash and send updated photos if it changes. Go to urgent care/ER now for facial swelling, breathing issues, severe pain, purple/non-blanching rash, or mucosal involvement.

messageReplies.name: Vaccine record request
messageReplies.text: Vaccine record for [PATIENT_NAME] has been prepared and can be shared through the patient portal.

messageReplies.name: School note response
messageReplies.text: A school note for [PATIENT_NAME] has been completed and sent. Please let us know if your school requires a specific format.

refillTemplates.instructions: Take 1 tablet by mouth each morning.
Inhale prescribed puffs BID with spacer.

schoolForms.name: Fever-free return
schoolForms.text: [PATIENT_NAME] may return to school/daycare once fever-free for 24 hours without fever-reducing medication and otherwise clinically improving.

schoolForms.name: Post-viral return guidance
schoolForms.text: [PATIENT_NAME] may return when energy and oral intake are improving and no new concerning symptoms are present.

priorAuth.text: Controller medication is medically necessary due to persistent asthma symptoms and to reduce exacerbation risk, urgent care use, and oral steroid exposure.
Medication is medically necessary for ADHD symptom control and functional school/home performance after clinical evaluation and ongoing follow-up.
